title: Recast the task as within-driver-race ranking, not only binary classification
summary: Each driver-race sequence has only a few true stop moments, so learning relative urgency within a group matches the decision structure.
rationale: Each driver-race sequence has only a few true stop moments, so learning relative urgency within a group matches the decision structure. XGBoost and CatBoost ranking docs explicitly support binary relevance inside query groups.
implementation_hint: Group rows by Driver x Race x Year, try `rank:map` or `rank:ndcg` in XGBoost and CatBoost pairwise/query losses with group ids, then blend the ranking score with the current probabilistic classifier. Evaluate on race-held-out folds, not random row CV.
expected_effect: Often lifts AUC by learning which laps should rise to the top of each stint/race even when absolute calibration is weak.
risk: Some groups may contain zero positives or multiple adjacent positives; ranking objectives can be noisy unless groups and sample weights are chosen carefully.
